body:
Commit 1:
- Diagnostic menu order change (For users, For developers, Available diagnostics)
- For users: example of using the @diagnostic_compute macro
- For users: julia snippet, no longer multiple 1., last snippet was wrong
- For developers: julia snippet, various bug fixes (e.g., _ escaped with \_) 
- For developers: example of using the @diagnostic_compute macro
- For developers: explained what @with_errors do, removed old version 

Commit 2:
- Adds a test for ALL_DIAGNOSTICS (check if it is a Dict and if its length change after adding a diagnostic variable)

https://clima.github.io/ClimaLand.jl/previews/PR743/
title: Update diagnostics docs

tag v0.14.3


Note, the branch name is v0.14.3, with dots, is this OK? Just making sure
title: tag v0.14.3

body:
Working on ClimaLand docs 1 day a month - Alexis August 23

https://clima.github.io/ClimaLand.jl/previews/PR736/

- [x] index: explain we have docs "for users" and "for developers"
- [x] index: explain that we use SI units
- [x] getting started: discoverability and help of ClimaLand, tree of package structure (submodules, exported functions and type), how to get info
- [x] reorganize a bit: API now last, tutorials after getting-started, etc.

!! Note: `about(ClimaLand)` in getting-started does not show functions, modules and type by colors in julia 1.10 (it will in julia 1.11), so the line below mentioning what the color mean looks a bit weird, but julia 1.11 should be here soon.
title: Alexis docs hackathon #1 Aug 23
